GPT4o-Receipt: A Dataset and human study for
AI-Generated Document Forensics Yan Zhang∗, Simiao Ren∗†, Ankit Raj, En Wei, Dennis Ng, Alex Shen, Jiayue Xu, Yuxin Zhang, Evelyn Marotta
∗Equal contribution †Corresponding author: benren@scam.ai Abstract—Can humans detect AI-generated financial documents Detector Calibration:
better than machines? We present GPT4o-Receipt, a benchmark Recall vs. FPR
of 1,235 receipt images pairing GPT-4o-generated receipts with
authentic ones from established datasets, evaluated by five state- 1.0
of-the-art multimodal LLMs and a 30-annotator crowdsourced Grok 4 Claude Sonnet 4
perceptual study. Gemini 2.5 Flash Better
Our findings reveal a striking paradox: humans are better at 0.8
seeing AI artifacts, yet worse at detecting AI documents. Human
Human2026 annotators exhibit the largest visual discrimination gap of any (Recall)
evaluator, yet their binary detection F1 falls well below Claude 0.6 GPT-5 Nano Sonnet 4 and below Gemini 2.5 Flash.
title: GPT4o-Receipt: A Dataset and Human Study for AI-Generated Document Forensics
